varieties.name: Eucalyptus resinifera (Red Mahogany)
varieties.origin: Native to eastern Australia (NSW to Queensland), 🌏 Indigenous
varieties.traits: Medium to tall tree, 40-45m height; rough stringy reddish-brown bark; lance-shaped glossy green leaves
varieties.availability: Queensland forestry suppliers, native plant nurseries

varieties.name: Eucalyptus resinifera subsp. hemilampra
varieties.origin: Taree NSW to Gladstone Queensland, 🌏 Indigenous
varieties.traits: Northern Queensland form of Red Mahogany; adapted to warmer subtropical conditions
varieties.availability: Queensland Forestry, Brisbane region native nurseries

varieties.name: Eucalyptus pellita (Large-fruited Red Mahogany)
varieties.origin: North Queensland (Townsville to Cape York) and scattered areas Gladstone to southern NSW, 🌏 Indigenous
varieties.traits: Similar to E. resinifera; reddish brown to brown bark; larger fruit capsules
varieties.availability: Queensland forestry programs, specialist eucalypt nurseries

varieties.name: Swietenia mahagoni (West Indian Mahogany)
varieties.traits: Semi-evergreen, 30-35m tall (up to 45m); rounded crown; small leaflets; drops leaves early spring

varieties.name: Swietenia macrophylla (Honduras Mahogany)
varieties.origin: Mexico to Amazon Brazil, commercial cultivation since 1800s
varieties.traits: Large evergreen tree, 30-40m tall (up to 60m); straight trunk; compound leaves with larger leaflets than S. mahagoni
varieties.growing_notes: CITES Appendix II protected; fast-growing; reaches harvest age 30-40 years
varieties.availability: Limited - specialist timber plantation suppliers, botanical institutions

varieties.name: Khaya senegalensis (African Mahogany - Dry Zone)
varieties.origin: Tropical Africa (Senegal to Uganda), introduced to Australia
varieties.traits: Fast-growing, 15-30m tall; deciduous; dark grey-brown bark; pinnate leaves; fragrant white flowers
varieties.availability: North Queensland plantation nurseries, Ross Creek Tropicals (ships to QLD)

varieties.name: Toona ciliata var. australis (Australian Red Cedar)
varieties.origin: Eastern Australia (Queensland to NSW), 🌏 Indigenous, 'Red Gold' of colonial era
varieties.traits: Deciduous, 30-40m tall (up to 60m); straight trunk in forest; compound leaves with red new growth; scaly grey-brown bark
varieties.availability: Queensland native plant nurseries, Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery, rainforest restoration projects

varieties.name: Didymocheton fraserianus (Rosewood/Rose Mahogany)
varieties.origin: Eastern Australia (Bundaberg QLD to Wyong NSW), 🌏 Indigenous
varieties.traits: Medium to large tree, up to 57m; strong rose scent from bark; slow-growing initially then fast with proper conditions
varieties.availability: Queensland rainforest nurseries, council tree programs

varieties.name: Eucalyptus acmenoides (White Mahogany/Barayly)
varieties.traits: Large tree up to 50m tall; grey to reddish-brown stringy bark; lance-shaped leaves with distinct pale underside

varieties.name: Melia azedarach (White Cedar/Chinaberry)
varieties.origin: Native to Australia and South East Asia, 🌏 Indigenous to northern/eastern Australia
varieties.traits: Deciduous shade tree, 7-12m tall (up to 30m); rounded crown; fragrant pale purple/lilac flowers; yellow fruits
varieties.availability: Common in Brisbane parks/streets, native plant nurseries

varieties.name: Swietenia macrophylla (Indian Mahogany)
varieties.origin: Central/South America; introduced India 1865; naturalized in Kerala
varieties.traits: Large evergreen, 30-40m tall; straight trunk; broad compound leaves; adaptable to tropical conditions
varieties.availability: Kerala state nurseries, timber plantation suppliers, widely available in Wayanad region

varieties.name: Swietenia mahagoni (West Indian Mahogany)
varieties.origin: Caribbean; introduced India 1865 from West Indies; naturalized
varieties.traits: Semi-evergreen, 30-35m tall; smaller leaves than S. macrophylla; rounded dense crown
varieties.availability: Kerala forestry departments, Tamil Nadu nurseries, plantation suppliers

varieties.name: Khaya senegalensis (African Mahogany)
varieties.origin: Tropical Africa; widely planted in India for plantations
varieties.traits: Fast-growing, 15-30m tall; deciduous; fragrant white flowers; dense rounded crown
varieties.availability: Karnataka nurseries (Sri Sai Forestry), Maharashtra, Gujarat suppliers, IndiaMART vendors

varieties.name: Khaya ivorensis (African Mahogany - Lagos/Ivory Coast)
varieties.origin: West/Central Africa (Côte d'Ivoire, Ghana, Nigeria); introduced Asia for plantations
varieties.traits: Large tree 40-50m; buttressed trunk; white flowers; evergreen to semi-deciduous
varieties.availability: Karnataka forestry nurseries, imported from Malaysian/Indonesian plantation stock

varieties.name: Khaya grandifoliola (African Mahogany - Large-leaved)
varieties.origin: Tropical Africa (Benin, Ghana, Nigeria, Sudan); increasingly planted in India
varieties.traits: 32-40m tall; large compound leaves; twisted/crooked bole in natural forests; straighter in plantations
varieties.availability: Emerging in Indian market, Karnataka plantation suppliers, specialty forestry nurseries

varieties.name: Khaya anthotheca (East African Mahogany)
varieties.origin: East Africa (Guinea to Tanzania, south to Mozambique); planted in tropical Asia
varieties.traits: 13-30m tall (up to 45m); buttressed base; pinnate leaves; deciduous or evergreen depending on climate
varieties.availability: Tropical Asia plantation suppliers, specialty African mahogany nurseries

varieties.name: Toona ciliata (Indian Cedar/Toon)
varieties.origin: South Asia from Afghanistan to Papua New Guinea, 🌏 Indigenous to India
varieties.traits: Fast-growing deciduous tree, 20-35m tall (up to 60m); compound leaves up to 40cm; smooth bark becoming rough with age
varieties.availability: Common in Kerala Forest Research Institute, state forestry nurseries, widely available throughout India

varieties.name: Cedrela odorata (Spanish Cedar/Cigar-box Cedar)
varieties.origin: Tropical America (Mexico to Argentina); introduced to Africa and Asia
varieties.traits: Semi-deciduous, 10-30m tall (up to 40m); pinnate compound leaves; aromatic wood with insect-repelling resin
varieties.availability: Introduced to Kenya, Uganda, Tanzania; available from African forestry programs; emerging in Asian market

varieties.name: Melia azedarach (Persian Lilac/Bakain)
varieties.origin: South Asia origin (India, Pakistan), 🌏 Indigenous to India
varieties.traits: Deciduous, 7-12m tall (up to 30m); fragrant lilac flowers in axillary panicles; yellow-brown fruits
varieties.availability: Common throughout Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Karnataka; widely available in Indian nurseries; naturalized forests
